title: DRACOCEPHALUM L. GENUS SPECIES ESSENTIAL OIL ANALYSIS
author: Sunnatullayeva, Mushtariy
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The genus Dracocephalum L. is an important medicinal plant in the flora of Central Asia, including Uzbekistan. This article provides a detailed analysis of the chemical composition of essential oils, extraction methods, and innovative approaches for the main species of the genus ( D. diversifolium  Rupr.,  D. integrifolium  Bunge,  D. kotschyi  Boiss.). Based on literature data and GC-MS analyses, tables show the yield of essential oils, their major compounds, and the efficiency of innovative methods.
steps_raw: The genus Dracocephalum L. is an important medicinal plant in the flora of Central Asia, including Uzbekistan. This article provides a detailed analysis of the chemical composition of essential oils, extraction methods, and innovative approaches for the main species of the genus ( D. diversifolium  Rupr.,  D. integrifolium  Bunge,  D. kotschyi  Boiss.). Based on literature data and GC-MS analyses, tables show the yield of essential oils, their major compounds, and the efficiency of innovative methods.
keywords: Dracocephalum L., genus, species, essential oils, GC-MS, supercritical CO₂ extraction (SFE), microwave-assisted hydrodistillation (MAHD), RSM, innovative methods.
